notes.note: The province of Gallia Narbonensis (southern Gaul), held by Lepidus; Furnius had reported that to fight there meant either abandoning the provincials or risking a dangerous engagement against Lepidus's forces.

notes.anchor: the concord of Plancus and Brutus
notes.note: After Antony's defeat at Mutina, Plancus's army and Decimus Brutus's army joined in Cisalpine Gaul; their cooperation was the republicans' best hope against Antony and Lepidus.

notes.anchor: if the elections fall in Sextilis
notes.note: Sextilis was the Roman month later renamed August; Cicero is teasing Furnius about racing home for the praetorian elections instead of staying at his post.

notes.anchor: whether you become praetor at this election or the next
notes.note: The praetorship was an annual elective magistracy on the cursus honorum; Cicero argues that the timing of the office matters far less than the lasting glory Furnius is earning in the war.

notes.anchor: we are pushing back into the month of January
notes.note: Cicero says he and his allies are working to postpone the praetorian elections, so that Furnius need not choose between his command and his candidacy.
